title: Classic 4-Cubby Nightstand
summary: A small four-opening cubby unit sized as a nightstand — same face-frame-and-1x12-box family as this catalog's larger cubby bookshelves, scaled down with optional adjustable shelves.
description:
This is the smallest member of this catalog's cubby-series family, built at nightstand scale with a 2x2 grid of four openings instead of the larger nine-cubby versions. The box goes together first from 1x12s, with either pocket holes or simple countersunk screws and glue — a genuinely flexible build for whatever tools you have on hand.

Shelves have a real choice built in: fix them permanently with pocket holes, or drill for shelf pins and leave them adjustable — and if you go adjustable, **don't nail through the face frame into the shelves**, since a nailed-through adjustable shelf isn't actually adjustable anymore. The optional side notch-outs are a styling detail that can be added at this stage or skipped and revisited later, since they don't affect the box's structural assembly either way.

Like this catalog's other double-face-framed cubby piece, this one gets a face frame on the back as well as the front — attach the back frame first, then the front, so the second frame goes onto a carcass that's already square rather than compounding two independent alignments. Optional base moulding, mitered at the corners, dresses up the bottom if you want a more finished, less construction-lumber look.

tools.note: Mitered corners on optional base moulding.

materials.name: Pocket hole screws, 1-1/4", or self-tapping star bit screws, 2"
Primer and paint, or stain and poly

steps.title: Build the box
steps.body: Build the box from the sides, center divider, top, and bottom shelf using 3/4" pocket holes and 1-1/4" pocket hole screws, or 2" countersunk screws and glue.
materials: Pocket hole screws, 1-1/4", or self-tapping star bit screws, 2"

steps.title: Add the shelves
steps.body: Add the shelves fixed with pocket holes, or drilled for shelf pins if you want them adjustable. Optional decorative side notch-outs can be cut now or later.

steps.title: Add the back face frame
steps.body: Build and attach the back face frame first.

steps.title: Add the front face frame and finish
steps.body: Build and attach the front face frame. If your shelves are adjustable, do not nail through the frame into the shelves. Add optional mitered base moulding. Sand and finish.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

unresolvedImages.alt: Small four-cubby nightstand with double face frames
